title: Living in Boulder vlog 7 -  9 -  2018: Gunna attach images to my history book pages

enrichment.summary: Kyle shares his plans while waiting for a new, faster computer to be delivered. He describes a project to add Google Earth screenshots and other images to his audio history book web pages, which are designed to be read aloud by Natural Reader software on mobile.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: excited about the 30% speed increase (2.8 to 3.9 GHz) which will help with his large web pages
believes it would be the best place on the internet to read/listen to history books once images are added
unsure whether to continue current history page organization work or immediately start adding images, leaning toward finishing current work first

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today is the day of getting my new computer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I just got a phone call from an 800 number, and I hope that wasn't Amazon telling me they were coming and now they're not.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't have my phone with me, so I don't remember to answer the phone when it comes, so I can be here when my computer arrives.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have a 2-year-old computer now, which was replaced about 3 years ago.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My current computer was the best laptop that money could buy when I got it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My current computer has 2.8 gigahertz, which shows how fast it can go.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The new computer goes up to 3.9 gigahertz, which is a 30% increase from 2.8.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm really looking forward to seeing how quickly the new computer can open up my pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to fix the links on my website the other day but couldn't open up a page, so I definitely want the new computer to handle big pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Finally, computers are the same size, which I need for the type of web pages I make.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think all these years other people were making web pages to suit slow computers, while I was making one page to suit past computers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's been like 6 months or 6 weekdays since I did my last video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was working on my free audio history book pages on my website and organizing files to mirror my computer to my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got stuck on Greece for a long time and am now kind of burned out on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to try a new project now, which is completing the audio books on my web page.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I was listening to Strava on my cell phone using Natural Reader, which reads web pages aloud.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Natural Reader has its own browser, and you can surf the web and hit play to have it read the page.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website is set up perfectly for Natural Reader because it reads big pages and highlights the chapter it's reading.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to add pictures of all the books I have, like Google Earth pictures of the places they're talking about, along with pictures of people and statues.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: On the cell phone, you can double-click the side to bring up the entire screen, so if I have photos there, you can tap to see them while listening.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to line the whole thing with photos, at least the ones I already have on Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might have to do screen captures of Google Earth images and put them on Flickr to fill up my pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I do that, it could open up another project where I have to do a screen capture of every single thing in Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once I add the photos, my audio books will be perfect and the best books to read stuff on the internet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might start the photo project immediately or work on my history book pages a little longer until I burn out and move on to it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to put a couple of weeks into the photo project to see how far I get and how much more work is needed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The project doesn't seem too time-consuming if the computer can handle the page, as you have to flip back and forth between the code page and the preview.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A slow computer could make the process take too long, but the new computer might work well for it.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log.
And it is for the day of July 9th, Monday, 1-2pm, 1-12pm.
Today is the day I'm getting my new computer.
I just got a phone call from an 800 number.
I hope that wasn't Amazon telling me they were coming.
And now they're not.
I'm going to have to remember to...
I don't have my phone with me.
I want to remember to answer the phone when it comes so I can be here.
When my computer comes...
I have like a two-year-old computer now.
I bought it three years ago, but they replaced it two years ago.
And I think it was the best computer, the best laptop that money can buy.
And then they replaced it two years ago, so it's two years old.
But it has the 2.8 gigabytes or gigahertz.
Which, I guess, is how much shows you how fast it can go.
And the new one goes up to, it's 3.9.
That's like a 30% increase or something like that.
So I'm really looking forward to seeing how quickly it can open up my pages.
I wanted to fix on this computer the other day just to see, to try it.
I wanted to try to fix the links on Libby.
I couldn't do it.
I couldn't open up Libby, so yeah, I definitely want to open up these big pages.
Finally, computers are the same size as I need them to be for the types of webpages I make.
Like all these years, other people were making webpages to suit slow computers.
And I was making webpages to suit fast computers, but I made it so it took forever because my computer couldn't handle it.
Also, you know, fast internet.
But yeah, so I'm doing this video.
It's been like six months, six week days since I did my last video.
But since then, I was working on, like, my free audio history book pages on my website and organizing the files to make it mirror my computer to my website.
And I'm still working on that.
I got stuck on grease for a long time.
And yeah, so I'm kind of burned out on it.
And so, anyways, I'm going to try to do a new project now.
That's what I'm doing in the video.
It's the video to talk about my new project.
It's the project of basically completing the audiobooks on my webpage.
Today I was listening to Strabo on my cell phone on my website with Natural Reader.
And Natural Reader, like, they have their own browser.
So you open up the Natural Reader, which is the program that reads to you, and then you just surf the web.
And then you can just hit play and it'll read it.
So that's what it was doing with my website.
And my website is set up perfectly for Natural Reader because it reads the big pages, but then there's two ways you can look at it.
And so when you look at it the normal way, like how your website looks.
And it has highlighted the chapter it's reading.
But it also shows on either side the place where I have reserved for the pictures.
And so I want to put the pictures of all of the books that I have.
Like Google Earth pictures of what they're talking about, where they're talking about.
You know, and then of course along with pictures of the people, you know, the statues and stuff.

So because on the cell phone, I don't have my cell phone with me, but you look at the cell phone and all you have to do is click, double click the side and it brings it up to the entire screen of the cell phone.
So if I have photos there, you could be listening to your cell phone about some really long book and then you get to an interesting part and you're like, wait, what?
All you have to do is take out the phone, look, tap on the picture and boom, you're looking at the photo of what you're talking about and where it is.
And then if you wanted to get into the photo album of that stuff, you know, you can go into Flickr from that.
But yeah, so I just want to align the whole thing with photos, at least the ones that I have, because I already have a lot of photos on Google Earth of ancient town.
I think I do.
Maybe I don't because I already have them on Google Earth, but I thought I did the screen captures and put them on Flickr.
But if I didn't, yeah, all I have to do is do the screen captures of them that I find.
But that could open up another project, because then all of a sudden now I have to do a screen capture of every single thing that I have on Google Earth.
But yeah, if I do that, then I can retrieve it from Flickr and put it on, fill up my pages.
And then my page, my books, audiobooks will be perfect.
They'll be the best books to read stuff on the Internet, for sure.
So I think I might start that immediately or just work on my, what I'm doing now for writer history book pages for a little bit until I burn out and then move over into that.
Just to put a couple weeks into it, just to see how far I get along with it, to see how much more work I need to finish it.
But it doesn't seem like it's that time consuming if the computer can handle the page, you know.

Because you have to flip back and forth between the code page, which is where you would paste the code for the pictures on Flickr, and then the WYSIWYG, like the part where you see it how it is, where you would then, you know, if you wanted to go to the other side, you know, back and forth.
So the computer could take too long if it's too slow.
But yeah, with this new page, picture, computer, it might work.
So I'm looking forward to that.
Okay, I think that's enough talking for now.
Peace out.
